title: Dinner

shortDialogue.speaker: 세경
shortDialogue.korean: 내일 퇴근 후에 나랑 같이 가자.
shortDialogue.english: Let's go together after work tomorrow.

longDialogue.speaker: 현우
longDialogue.english: No, nothing. I wanted to have a drink.

longDialogue.speaker: 세경
longDialogue.korean: 그래? 내일 우리도 술 한잔할 거야. 너도 와!
longDialogue.english: Really? We are also going to have a drink tomorrow. You should come, too!

longDialogue.speaker: 세경
longDialogue.korean: 응, 와도 돼. 선영이도 괜찮을 거야.
longDialogue.english: Yeah, you can come. I think Seon-yeong will be fine with that.

longDialogue.speaker: 현우
longDialogue.english: Sounds good. Then, where should I go?

longDialogue.speaker: 세경
longDialogue.korean: 내일 퇴근 후에 나랑 같이 가자.
longDialogue.english: Let's go together after work tomorrow.

longDialogue.speaker: 세경
longDialogue.korean: 선영이는 일곱 시까지 술집으로 올 거야.
longDialogue.english: Seon-yeong will come to the pub by 7.

culturalTip: When native Korean speakers suggest having a drink, they use the expression "한잔하다". When translated literally, 한 means "one", 잔 means "glass" (and is also used as a counter for alcohol), and 하다 means "to do" or "to have". Therefore, they say, "to have one glass" as a suggestion. Let's have a glass!
koreanReview:
- Short Dialogue
현우: 내일 어디로 가면 돼?
세경: 내일 퇴근 후에 나랑 같이 가자.

- Long Dialogue
현우: 세경아, 내일 바빠?
세경: 내일 선영이 만나.
현우: 그래?
세경: 응. 왜? 무슨 일 있어?
현우: 아니, 그냥. 술 한잔하고 싶어서.
세경: 그래? 내일 우리도 술 한잔할 거야. 너도 와!
현우: 그래도 돼?
세경: 응, 와도 돼. 선영이도 괜찮을 거야.
현우: 좋아. 그럼 어디로 가면 돼?
세경: 내일 퇴근 후에 나랑 같이 가자.
현우: 선영이는?
세경: 선영이는 일곱 시까지 술집으로 올 거야.

coreVocab.meaning: to just have to + VERB, to be just supposed to + VERB, can just + VERB

extendedVocab.meaning: to be ok, to be alright

grammar.title: Giving Permission
grammar.pattern: VERB + -아/어/여도 돼(요) = you can VERB / it is ok to VERB
grammar.meaning: you can VERB / it is ok to VERB

examples.english: You can come. / It is ok for you to come.

exercises.english: You can have a drink. / It is ok for you to have a drink.
You can go/leave. / It is ok for you to leave.

grammar.title: Asking about the Requirements/Instructions When Given a Task
grammar.pattern: VERB + -(으)면 돼(요)? : I just have to VERB? / So, all I have to do is VERB? / Is it ok if I just VERB?
grammar.meaning: I just have to VERB? / So, all I have to do is VERB? / Is it ok if I just VERB?
grammar.explanation:
* If a verb stem ends with a vowel, attach -면 돼(요)? at the end, and if it ends with a consonant, use -으면 돼(요)?
** There is no set translation of this phrase as the English meaning changes depending on the context of the sentence.

examples.korean: 가다 + -(으)면 돼(요)? → 가면 돼(요)? = I just have to go?

exercises.english: So, all I have to do is meet?
Is it ok if I just do (it)?

pronunciation.rule: When a syllable ends with ㄶ and is followed by a syllable that begins with a vowel, only the ㄴ is pronounced, while the ㅎ is not pronounced.
When a syllable ends with ㄴ and is followed by a syllable that begins with a vowel, ㄴ becomes part of the following syllable rather than being pronounced distinctly as two different syllables.
